% === Constants ===

output_rows(24). output_cols(24).

% === Derived predicates ===
% Define tile coordinates: each 4x4 tile
tile_row(R, TR) :- R = 0..23, TR = R // 4.
tile_col(C, TC) :- C = 0..23, TC = C // 4.

% A cell (R,C) belongs to tile (TR,TC)
tile(R, C, TR, TC) :- R = 0..23, C = 0..23, TR = R // 4, TC = C // 4.

% 3x3 block centered at (R,C) — only valid for R,C in 1..21
three_by_three(R, C) :- R = 1..21, C = 1..21.

% Uniform 3x3 block: all 9 cells have same non-zero color
uniform_3x3(R, C, Color) :- 
    three_by_three(R, C),
    input(R, C, Color),
    Color != 0,
    #count { R1, C1 : R1 = R-1..R+1, C1 = C-1..C+1, input(R1, C1, Color) } = 9.

% 3x3 block is isolated (surrounded by 0 or 8) — at least 8 neighbors are 0 or 8
isolated_3x3(R, C) :- 
    three_by_three(R, C),
    #count { DR, DC : DR = -1..1, DC = -1..1, DR != 0; DC != 0, 
             R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, input(R2, C2, 0) } >= 8,
    #count { DR, DC : DR = -1..1, DC = -1..1, DR != 0; DC != 0, 
             R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, input(R2, C2, 8) } >= 8.

% 3x3 block is adjacent to 8 in input
adjacent_to_8(R, C) :- 
    three_by_three(R, C),
    #count { DR, DC : DR = -1..1, DC = -1..1, DR != 0; DC != 0, 
             R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, input(R2, C2, 8) } > 0.

% 3x3 block has color 3, 4, 6, or 8
is_tile_color(Color) :- color(Color), Color != 0, Color != 8.

% Identify all 3x3 blocks in input with color 3, 4, 6, 8
tile_block(R, C, Color) :- 
    three_by_three(R, C), 
    uniform_3x3(R, C, Color),
    is_tile_color(Color).

% Identify 3x3 blocks that are in the same tile as a 3x3 block of color 6
same_tile_6(R, C) :- 
    tile_block(R, C, 6),
    tile(R, C, TR, TC),
    #count { R1, C1 : tile(R1, C1, TR, TC), tile_block(R1, C1, 6) } > 0.

% === Output domain ===
% All output cells
output_cell(R, C) :- R = 0..23, C = 0..23.

% Each output cell gets exactly one color
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% === Integrity constraints ===
% Preserve background (0) and border (8) cells
:- output(R, C, Color), input(R, C, 0), Color != 0.
:- output(R, C, Color), input(R, C, 8), Color != 8.

% 3x3 blocks of color 3, 4, 6, 8 must appear only if they existed in input
:- output(R, C, Color), is_tile_color(Color), 
   not three_by_three(R, C), 
   not input(R, C, Color).

% 3x3 blocks must be aligned to tile boundaries: top-left corner at (4*TR + 1, 4*TC + 1)
:- output(R, C, Color), is_tile_color(Color), 
   three_by_three(R, C),
   (R mod 4) != 1, (C mod 4) != 1.

% 3x3 blocks must not extend beyond grid
:- output(R, C, Color), is_tile_color(Color), 
   R+2 > 23, C+2 > 23.

% 3x3 blocks must not overlap
:- output(R1, C1, Color), output(R2, C2, Color), 
   R1 != R2; C1 != C2,
   three_by_three(R1, C1), three_by_three(R2, C2),
   R1 >= R2, R1 <= R2+2, C1 >= C2, C1 <= C2+2.

% 3x3 blocks that were adjacent to 8 in input must be adjacent to 8 in output
:- output(R, C, Color), is_tile_color(Color), 
   three_by_three(R, C),
   adjacent_to_8(R, C),
   #count { DR, DC : DR = -1..1, DC = -1..1, DR != 0; DC != 0, 
            R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, output(R2, C2, 8) } = 0.

% 3x3 blocks of color 6 must be placed only in tiles that had a 3x3 block of color 6 in input
:- output(R, C, 6), three_by_three(R, C), 
   not same_tile_6(R, C).

% 3x3 blocks must be surrounded by 8 in output if they were adjacent to 8 in input
:- output(R, C, Color), is_tile_color(Color), 
   three_by_three(R, C),
   adjacent_to_8(R, C),
   #count { DR, DC : DR = -1..1, DC = -1..1, DR != 0; DC != 0, 
            R2 = R+DR, C2 = C+DC, output(R2, C2, 8) } = 0.

% 3x3 blocks of color 3, 4, 6, 8 must not be placed unless they were present in input
:- output(R, C, Color), is_tile_color(Color), 
   three_by_three(R, C),
   not input(R, C, Color).

% === Constants ===
color(0). color(1). color(2). color(5).
input_rows(10). input_cols(10).
output_rows(10). output_cols(10).

% === Auxiliary predicates ===
% Identify the base row (row with all 5s)
base_row(R) :- input(R, C, 5), #count { C2 : input(R, C2, 5) } = input_cols.

% Identify columns that contain color 2 in input
col_with_2(C) :- input(R, C, 2).

% For each column with 2, find the lowest and highest row where 2 appears
lowest_2_row(C, R) :- col_with_2(C), input(R, C, 2), not (input(R2, C, 2), R2 > R).
highest_2_row(C, R) :- col_with_2(C), input(R, C, 2), not (input(R2, C, 2), R2 < R).

% Identify positions where color 1 appears in input
pos_1(R, C) :- input(R, C, 1).

% === Choice rules ===
% All output cells are assigned exactly one color
output_cell(R, C) :- R = 0..output_rows-1, C = 0..output_cols-1.
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% === Integrity constraints (rules) ===
% Preserve input 5 in base row: output must have 5 in base row only where input has 5
:- output(R, C, 5), base_row(R), input(R, C, 0).

% Preserve input 1: output must have 1 only where input has 1
:- output(R, C, 1), not pos_1(R, C).

% Preserve input 2: output must have 2 only where input has 2
:- output(R, C, 2), not input(R, C, 2).

% Color 5 must appear only in the base row
:- output(R, C, 5), not base_row(R).

% For each column with 2 in input, output must have 2 in all rows between lowest and highest (inclusive)
:- col_with_2(C), lowest_2_row(C, Low), highest_2_row(C, High), R = Low..High, not output(R, C, 2).

% For each column with 2 in input, output must not have 2 outside the range [lowest, highest]
:- col_with_2(C), lowest_2_row(C, Low), highest_2_row(C, High), output(R, C, 2), R < Low.
:- col_with_2(C), lowest_2_row(C, Low), highest_2_row(C, High), output(R, C, 2), R > High.

% Color 1 must appear only in the same column as input 1, and only in rows below the base row (i.e., rows 5 to 9)
:- output(R, C, 1), pos_1(R0, C), R < input_rows, R != R0.

% Color 1 must appear only in rows below the base row
:- output(R, C, 1), R < input_rows.

% Color 2 must appear only in columns with input 2
:- output(R, C, 2), not col_with_2(C).

% Color 2 must not appear above the base row unless input has it
:- output(R, C, 2), R < input_rows, not input(R, C, 2).

% Color 1 must not appear above the base row unless input has it
:- output(R, C, 1), R < input_rows, not pos_1(R, C).

% === Identify 3x3 blocks: non-zero, non-8 color, fully bounded by 8s ===
% A 3x3 block of color C starting at (R, C0) is valid if:
% - All 9 cells have color C
% - The block is surrounded by 8s on all sides (top, bottom, left, right)
% - C > 0 and C \= 8
block(R, C0, C) :-
    input(R, C0, C),
    input(R, C0+1, C), input(R, C0+2, C),
    input(R+1, C0, C), input(R+1, C0+1, C), input(R+1, C0+2, C),
    input(R+2, C0, C), input(R+2, C0+1, C), input(R+2, C0+2, C),
    C > 0, C \= 8,
    % Top row: above the block
    input(R-1, C0, 8), input(R-1, C0+1, 8), input(R-1, C0+2, 8),
    % Bottom row: below the block
    input(R+3, C0, 8), input(R+3, C0+1, 8), input(R+3, C0+2, 8),
    % Left column: to the left of the block
    input(R, C0-1, 8), input(R+1, C0-1, 8), input(R+2, C0-1, 8),
    % Right column: to the right of the block
    input(R, C0+3, 8), input(R+1, C0+3, 8), input(R+2, C0+3, 8),
    R+2 < 25, C0+2 < 24.

% === Define which blocks are moved: only those whose top row is at multiple of 5 ===
moved_block(R, C0, C) :-
    block(R, C0, C),
    R mod 5 = 0.

% === Define the target position for moved blocks: rightmost 3x3 slot in their row group (col 21) ===
% A target slot exists at row R (R mod 5 = 0) if:
% - The 3x3 area at (R, 21) is bounded by 8s
% - The 3x3 area at (R, 21) is currently empty (0) in input
target_slot(R) :-
    moved_block(R, _, _),
    % Check target slot is bounded by 8s
    input(R-1, 21, 8), input(R-1, 22, 8), input(R-1, 23, 8),
    input(R+3, 21, 8), input(R+3, 22, 8), input(R+3, 23, 8),
    input(R, 20, 8), input(R+1, 20, 8), input(R+2, 20, 8),
    input(R, 24, 8), input(R+1, 24, 8), input(R+2, 24, 8),
    % Check target slot is currently empty (0) in input
    input(R, 21, 0), input(R, 22, 0), input(R, 23, 0),
    input(R+1, 21, 0), input(R+1, 22, 0), input(R+1, 23, 0),
    input(R+2, 21, 0), input(R+2, 22, 0), input(R+2, 23, 0).

% === Constraints: enforce the transformation ===
% 1. For each moved block, its original position becomes 0 in output
:- moved_block(R, C0, C), R <= R1, R1 < R+3, C0 <= C1, C1 < C0+3, output(R1, C1, Color), Color != 0.

% 2. For each moved block, the target position becomes the block color
:- moved_block(R, C0, C), R <= R1, R1 < R+3, 21 <= C1, C1 < 24, output(R1, C1, Color), Color != C.

% 3. Only blocks with top row at multiple of 5 are moved
:- moved_block(R, _, _), R mod 5 != 0.

% 4. Target slot must exist for moved blocks
:- moved_block(R, _, _), not target_slot(R).

% 5. For non-block cells, output must match input
:- output_cell(R, C), not block(R, _, _), input(R, C, Color), output(R, C, OutputColor), OutputColor != Color.

% 6. For cells in moved block target area, output must be C (ensured by constraint 2)
% 7. For cells in moved block original area, output must be 0 (ensured by constraint 1)

% 8. Ensure that the target slot is not already occupied by a block of different color in input
% But we are allowed to overwrite, so no constraint needed

% 9. Ensure that the target slot is not already occupied by a block of the same color in input
% But we are allowed to overwrite, so no constraint needed

% 10. Ensure that only one block is placed in the target slot (but each row group has only one target slot, so no conflict)

% === Colors (hardcoded) ===
color(0..9).

% === Step 1: Determine input bounding box ===
% Find all rows and columns that contain non-zero values
row(R) :- input(R, _, Color), Color != 0.
col(C) :- input(_, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Compute min and max row and column
min_row(MR) :- MR = #min{ R : row(R) }.
max_row(MR) :- MR = #max{ R : row(R) }.
min_col(MC) :- MC = #min{ C : col(C) }.
max_col(MC) :- MC = #max{ C : col(C) }.

% Compute output dimensions: extend bounding box by 2 in each direction
% Ensure at least 2x2 output grid
output_height(H) :- H = #max{ 2, (max_row - min_row + 1) + 2 }.
output_width(W) :- W = #max{ 2, (max_col - min_col + 1) + 2 }.

% Generate all output cell positions
output_cell(R, C) :- output_height(H), output_width(W), R = 0..H-1, C = 0..W-1.

% === Step 2: Define output frame (border) ===
% Top and bottom rows
output_frame_cell(R, C) :- output_height(H), output_width(W), R = 0 ; R = H-1, C = 0..W-1.
% Left and right columns (excluding corners already covered)
output_frame_cell(R, C) :- output_height(H), output_width(W), C = 0 ; C = W-1, R = 1..H-2.

% Frame must be color 4
1 { output(R, C, 4) } 1 :- output_frame_cell(R, C).

% === Step 3: Internal region must be non-zero ===
% Internal region: rows 1 to H-2, cols 1 to W-2
internal_cell(R, C) :- output_height(H), output_width(W), R = 1..H-2, C = 1..W-2.

% Internal cells must be non-zero
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color), Color != 0 } 1 :- internal_cell(R, C).

% === Step 4: Enforce vertical symmetry ===
% For every internal cell (R, C), the mirrored cell (H-1-R, C) must have the same color
% Use safe variable binding: H is known from output_height
% We need to ensure H is fixed before using it in arithmetic
% The symmetry constraint: output(R,C,Color) iff output(H-1-R,C,Color) for R < H-1-R
% We enforce that if (R,C) has color X, then (H-1-R,C) must have same color
:- output(R, C, Color), output(H-1-R, C, Color2), R < H-1-R, Color != Color2.

% === Step 5: Restrict output colors to 1,2,3,4 only ===
:- output(_, _, Color), Color != 0, Color != 1, Color != 2, Color != 3, Color != 4.

% === Step 6: Frame must be only on outer edges ===
:- output(R, C, 4), not output_frame_cell(R, C).

% === Step 7: Background (0) only allowed in frame ===
:- output(R, C, 0), not output_frame_cell(R, C).

% === Step 8: Final choice rule for all output cells ===
% Each output cell must have exactly one color
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).
